perturbation.original_text: For a period of two (2) years subsequent to the termination of discussions between the parties with respect to the Proposed Transaction, neither party shall, without prior written consent of the other party, directly or indirectly solicit for hire, any person currently employed by the other party (or any of its subsidiaries); provided, however, that the foregoing provision shall not prevent either party, without such consent, from employing any employee who (i) contacts the hiring party directly at his or her own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from the hiring party or (ii) responds to a mass media solicitation or advertisement consistent with the hiring party's past practices that is not directed at employees of the other party.
perturbation.changed_text: For a period of two (2) years subsequent to the termination of discussions between the parties with respect to the Proposed Transaction, neither party shall, without prior written consent of the other party, directly or indirectly solicit for hire, any person currently employed by the other party (or any of its subsidiaries); provided, however, that the foregoing provision shall prevent either party, without such consent, from employing any employee who (i) contacts the hiring party directly at his or her own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from the hiring party or (ii) responds to a mass media solicitation or advertisement consistent with the hiring party's past practices that is not directed at employees of the other party.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall not prevent' to 'shall prevent' the clause now contradicts itself. It initially states that neither party can solicit employees, but the 'provided however' clause originally created exceptions. Now, these 'exceptions' are blocked creating a contradiction in the enforcement, as it prohibits employing employees who contact the hiring party. This in-text contradiction significantly alters the hiring restrictions and creates an in-text contradiction within the same paragraph.
perturbation.contradicted_text: For a period of two (2) years subsequent to the termination of discussions between the parties with respect to the Proposed Transaction, neither party shall, without prior written consent of the other party, directly or indirectly solicit for hire, any person currently employed by the other party (or any of its subsidiaries); provided, however, that the foregoing provision shall prevent either party, without such consent, from employing any employee who (i) contacts the hiring party directly at his or her own initiative without any direct or indirect solicitation by or encouragement from the hiring party or (ii) responds to a mass media solicitation or advertisement consistent with the hiring party's past practices that is not directed at employees of the other party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters 'shall not prevent' to 'shall prevent'. This change directly contradicts the original intent of the clause by prohibiting the employment of individuals who contact the hiring party on their own initiative or respond to mass media solicitations. The contradicted text highlights that the hiring party is prevented from employing such individuals, directly conflicting with the initial statement that neither party shall solicit for hire, but allows exceptions for unsolicited contacts and responses to mass media. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' as different sections of the contract now provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: In that case, or in the event that the Disclosing Party, in its sole discretion, so requests or the Proposed Transaction is not consummated by the Receiving Party, the Receiving party shall, upon the Disclosing Party's written request, promptly deliver to the Disclosing Party all Evaluation Material, and, at the Receiving Party's election, return or destroy (provided that any such destruction shall be certified by a duly authorized Representative of the Receiving Party) all copies, reproductions, summaries, analyses or extracts thereof, including any electronic or computer file copies, or based thereon in the Receiving Party's possession or in the possession of any Representative of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: In that case, or in the event that the Disclosing Party, in its sole discretion, so requests or the Proposed Transaction is not consummated by the Receiving Party, the Receiving party shall, upon the Disclosing Party's written request, promptly deliver to the Disclosing Party all Evaluation Material, and the Receiving Party shall return or destroy (provided that any such destruction shall be certified by a duly authorized Representative of the Disclosing Party) all copies, reproductions, summaries, analyses or extracts thereof, including any electronic or computer file copies, or based thereon in the Receiving Party's possession or in the possession of any Representative of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing 'at the Receiving Party's election' and changing 'Representative of the Receiving Party' to 'Representative of the Disclosing Party', the clause now mandates destruction or return certified by the Disclosing Party. This creates an in-text contradiction. Previously, the Receiving Party had the option to return or destroy. Now, the Receiving Party no longer has a choice, and the destruction must be certified by the Disclosing Party's representative, altering control over Evaluation Material disposition. This change contradicts the initially agreed-upon rights and responsibilities regarding Evaluation Material.
perturbation.contradicted_text: All information (i) about the Disclosing Party or (ii) about a third party (which information was provided to the Disclosing Party subject to a confidentiality agreement with such third party) furnished by the Disclosing Party or its Representatives (as defined below) to the Receiving Party or its Representatives, whether furnished before or after the date hereof in connection with the Proposed Transaction, and regardless of the manner in which it is furnished, is referred to in this letter agreement as "Evaluation Material."
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the Receiving Party's election to return or destroy Evaluation Material and mandates that destruction be certified by the Disclosing Party's representative. This directly contradicts the original understanding of control over Evaluation Material disposition, creating an inconsistency in obligations. The contradicted text defines Evaluation Material, and the changed text alters the Receiving Party's rights regarding this material, thus there is a strong in-text contradiction. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall not be entitled to rely on the completeness of any Evaluation Material, but shall be entitled to rely solely on such representations and warranties regarding the completeness of the Evaluation Material as may be made to it in any definitive agreement relating to the Proposed Transaction, subject to the terms and conditions of such agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall be entitled to rely on the completeness of any Evaluation Material, but shall be entitled to rely solely on such representations and warranties regarding the completeness of the Evaluation Material as may be made to it in any definitive agreement relating to the Proposed Transaction, subject to the terms and conditions of such agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'shall not be entitled' to 'shall be entitled' creates an in-text contradiction. Initially, the Receiving Party could not rely on the completeness of Evaluation Material outside of definitive agreements. Now, the Receiving Party is entitled to rely on the completeness of any Evaluation Material, contradicting the intent of limiting reliance only to warranties within a final agreement. This creates ambiguity about the responsibility and liability related to the accuracy of provided information, creating an uncertainty in enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This letter agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties hereto concerning confidentiality of the Evaluation Material, and no modification of this letter agreement or waiver of the terms and conditions hereof shall be binding upon either party hereto, unless approved in writing by each such party.
perturbation.justification: The change in the text from 'shall not be entitled' to 'shall be entitled' directly contradicts the intention of the original agreement, which aimed to limit reliance on the Evaluation Material to only those representations and warranties made within a definitive agreement. The contradicted text explicitly states that 'this letter agreement contains the entire agreement...concerning confidentiality of the Evaluation Material'. By now allowing reliance on the Evaluation Material outside of a definitive agreement, the changed text introduces a contradiction regarding the scope of the agreement and the extent to which parties can rely on the provided information. This falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because different sections of the contract now provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: This letter agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Texas, without giving effect to its principles or rules regarding conflicts of laws, other than such principles directing application of Texas law.
perturbation.changed_text: This letter agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without giving effect to its principles or rules regarding conflicts of laws, other than such principles directing application of Texas law.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the governing law from Texas to Delaware creates a direct conflict. One clause specifies Texas law, while another implies Texas law shall direct. This in-text contradiction introduces significant uncertainty about which jurisdiction's laws will govern the contract, leading to ambiguity in enforcement, potential disputes, and a direct conflict regarding legal interpretation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This letter agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, without giving effect to its principles or rules regarding conflicts of laws, other than such principles directing application of Texas law.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that Texas law governs the agreement, with a clause stating that Texas law principles shall direct. The changed text modifies the governing law to Delaware but retains the clause referencing Texas law principles. The contradicted text is exactly the same as the changed text. This creates a direct contradiction because the agreement is now governed by Delaware law, but a clause still refers to applying Texas law principles, thus creating an inconsistency. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', where different sections of the contract create an in-text contradiction regarding which terms should be enforced.
